description: The nodev mount option can be used to prevent device files from being created in /boot. Legitimate character and block devices should exist only in the /dev directory on the root partition or within chroot jails built for system services. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot.
rationale:
The only legitimate location for device files is the <tt>/dev</tt> directory
located on the root partition. The only exception to this is chroot jails.
ocil_clause: the "/boot" file system does not have the "nodev" option set
ocil:
Verify the nodev option is configured for the /boot mount point, run the following command: 
$ sudo mount | grep '\s/boot\s'
. . . /boot . . . nodev . . .
fixtext: Modify "/etc/fstab" to use the "nodev" option on the "/boot" directory.
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must mount /boot with the nodev option.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must mount /boot with the nodev option.
stig.vuldiscussion: The only legitimate location for device files is the "/dev" directory located on the root partition. The only exception to this is chroot jails.
stig.checktext:
Verify that the "/boot" mount point has the "nodev" option with the following command:

$ mount | grep '\s/boot\s'

/dev/sda1 on /boot type xfs (rw,nodev,relatime,seclabel,attr2)

If the "/boot" file system does not have the "nodev" option set, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext: Modify "/etc/fstab" to use the "nodev" option on the "/boot" directory.

title: Add nodev Option to /boot
